message:
test(core): add mock-free tests for export, template, and semantic modules (bd-2yxy, bd-1qkc, bd-33z2)

Export module (2 files, 41 tests):
- json.test.ts: Registry payload structure, prompt list, metadata counts
- markdown.test.ts: Prompt/bundle/workflow markdown generation, sections, fencing

Template module (1 file, 38 tests):
- render.test.ts: Variable substitution, extraction, defaults, missing vars
  Also covers variables.ts: dynamic defaults, name formatting, placeholders

Search semantic (1 file, 30 tests):
- semantic.test.ts: Hash embeddings, cosine similarity, reranking, model state

Core now at 358 tests (up from 249), all passing, zero mocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reduce mock boilerplate — hooks should be tested against consistent, realistic data shapes, not ad-hoc fakes per test. BLOCKED BY bd-tr6k — need hook testing patterns established first.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:27Z"}]}

APPROACH: Provide real transcript data fixtures, render components, verify structure. Complex but testable — test with realistic session data.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:31:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256945535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:05:31.256921389Z","close_reason":"Spec complete in this repo; backend persistence/enforcement must be implemented in premium repo. Public repo follow-ups (CLI/web consuming /cli/budgets when available) should be tracked separately once endpoints land.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo):

Data model:
- budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at.
- budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at.
- usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at.

API (premium):
- GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }.
- PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload.
- GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI.
- POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional).

Enforcement:
- On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage.
- If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response).
- Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage).

Client integration notes (public repo):
- CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl.
- Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback.
- Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional spec details:

Response shapes:
- GET /cli/budgets -> { ok: true, data: { budgets: { monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd, alertsEnabled, hardStopEnabled }, usage: { month, totalCostUsd, totalTokens, lastRunAt } } }
- PUT /cli/budgets (body: partial budgets) -> same payload as GET.
- Error (budget hard stop): status 402 or 403 with { ok:false, error:{ code:"budget_exceeded", message, caps:{ monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd }, usage:{ month, totalCostUsd }, projected:{ totalCostUsd, inputTokens, outputTokens, model } } }.
- Warning only (per-run over cap, hard stop disabled): include { warning:{ code:"budget_warning", message, capUsd, totalCostUsd } } in normal response.

Rollups:
- usage_rollups should be updated on actual billable runs only.
- Use UTC month boundaries; month stored as YYYY-MM string for deterministic queries.

Client expectations:
- CLI/web should treat missing /cli/budgets endpoint as "not available" and keep local-only UI/alerts.
- CLI can show budget status in `jfp status` only when endpoint returns data; otherwise fallback to config-only summary.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01: all open beads already in_progress; continuing bd-1ppu. Remaining work is premium-repo implementation (endpoints + persistence + enforcement). Blocked here until premium repo mirror/assignment. Suggested next steps: mirror bead in premium repo; implement /cli/budgets + /cli/budget-alerts; enforce hard-stop on run endpoints; add usage rollups + tests.","created_at":"2026-02-01T22:57:22Z"},{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv triage 2026-02-01T23:23Z: top picks unchanged; all open beads already in_progress. Continuing bd-1ppu.

Concrete next steps (premium repo):
1) Add budgets table + budget_alerts + usage_rollups (UTC month key).
2) Implement GET/PUT /cli/budgets (auth + validate, return usage).
3) Implement GET /cli/budget-alerts (paging by before/limit).
4) Enforce hard stop in run/execute endpoints; return error code budget_exceeded with caps + usage + projected cost.
5) Add warning payload for per-run overages when hard stop disabled.
6) Tests: budget validation, rollups increment on actual usage, hard-stop block, warning path.

Public repo follow-up (after premium endpoints exist):
- Update CLI status to prefer /cli/budgets (fallback to local config).
- Wire web Budget Settings page to backend instead of localStorage; keep local-only fallback if endpoint missing.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:24:14Z"}]}

Need to: fix deps, integrate logging, verify ALL specs pass, add CLI E2E tests, create CI pipeline, add production smoke tests. 10 subtasks with critical path: fix deps → verify specs → CI → production.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:58Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background
Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts.

## Scope
- Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI).
- UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs.
- Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts.
- CLI: `jfp cost <prompt>` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility.
- UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts.
- CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging.
- Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Cost calculation across models and price tiers.
- Budget threshold logic and alert formatting.

### Integration Tests
- Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output.
- Budget updates persist and are read by CLI.

### E2E Tests
- Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI.

## Success Criteria
- Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage.
- Users can see cost before running prompts.
- Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:05:13.792240399Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:28.450652742Z","close_reason":"Implemented in 116012f (budget settings UI + CLI budget alerts); backend persistence/hard-stop tracked separately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Web UI cost badges implemented in commit fc824eb. CostBadge added to PromptCard and PromptDetailModal. Remaining: budget settings page and alert logging.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:33:35Z"},{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2026-01-31: Added CostBadge tests (commit 3e83b39). Web badges complete. Remaining work: budget settings page requires backend/database infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:46:06Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added budget alert log summary to jfp status (JSON + human output). Pending tests (requires explicit delete approval).","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:09:50Z"},{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Updated registry status tests to assert budgetAlerts in JSON output (tests not executed due to deletion guard).","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:11Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added CLI budget alert log viewer (jfp cost --alerts/--alerts-limit), web budget page import + alert history + CLI sync commands, and docs updates (README + CLI help + web CLI help). Tests not run due to deletion guard.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:05:13Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2yzg","title":"Test: stores batch 4 (roadmap, moderation, incident, support)","description":"Fourth batch of test coverage for complex stores:
- lib/roadmap/roadmap-store.ts (619 lines) - roadmap items, voting, milestones

 {"id":"bd-32rw","title":"Fix flakey CLI test timeout for deprecated skills commands","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T16:46:04.470668197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:55.467931042Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:55.467905073Z","close_reason":"Raised CLI suite per-test timeout to match spawnJfp and prevent SIGTERM flakes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-32rw","author":"QuietBasin","text":"Root cause: bun test default per-test timeout is 5000ms, but our CLI E2E helpers spawn bun run jfp.ts with 15-30s timeouts. Under parallel load, some spawn-based tests can exceed 5s and get SIGTERM (exit 143).

Fix: raise CLI suite timeout via test:cli/test:cli:coverage scripts (--timeout=30000) and add an explicit per-test timeout in packages/cli/__tests__/commands/skills.test.ts for extra robustness.","created_at":"2026-02-06T16:51:35Z"}]}
